danger_signals.visual_form: Impact effects, sprite flashing or knockback animation
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of control or speed penalty in racing/skiing events
Immediate failure state or significant score/time penalty

danger_signals.visual_form: Edge collision, loss of momentum visual feedback
danger_signals.meaning: Failed trick execution or loss of scoring opportunity
Damage taken in snowball fight minigame, position disadvantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Character maintaining stable sprite animation, continuous forward motion
safety_signals.meaning: Successful execution of skiing/boarding without incident

safety_signals.visual_form: Smooth character positioning at halfpipe base or mountain landing zone
safety_signals.meaning: Successful aerial maneuver completion, points awarded

safety_signals.visual_form: Centered on-screen position, predictable scrolling parallax
safety_signals.meaning: Controlled pace in inflatable sled racing event

safety_signals.visual_form: Open screen space ahead, minimal visual clutter in foreground
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage in downhill skiing event

faction_indicators.color_identity: Varies by player (likely bright primary colors for NES palette distinction)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opponent skiers/snowboarders rendered in contrasting color palette
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ski lodge/resort setting serves as player home base implied by Rodney Dangerfield shopkeeper presence

faction_indicators.color_identity: Alternate palette swaps from player character
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Different sprite colorations for each of up to 6 players in hotseat modes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Score/time display prominent on HUD, character sprites animating through event-specific terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters execute sport-specific movements: snowboarding tricks, skiing downslope, sled acceleration, aerial maneuvers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Static shop background with Rodney Dangerfield character model, shop interface displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Minigame selection available, player configuration for multiplayer hotseat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character tumble animation, potential fade-to-black or event restart prompt
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Loss of forward momentum, immediate retry or round conclusion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character airborne, terrain walls visible at screen edges, rotation/flip animation frames
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player input timing critical for trick success, visual feedback via landing smoothness

environmental_storytelling.example: Rodney Dangerfield shopkeeper presence establishes casual, comedic winter sports atmosphere rather than serious competitive tone

environmental_storytelling.technique: Minigame event variety as world expansion
environmental_storytelling.example: Five distinct events (halfpipe, sled racing, aerial skiing, downhill, snowball fights) suggest diverse resort facilities and recreational activities

environmental_storytelling.technique: Multiplayer hotseat support as social play indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Up to 6-player support in majority of minigames suggests shared resort experience, social competition context
Distinct environmental rendering for each minigame (halfpipe walls, downhill slopes, arena for snowball fights) communicates localized play spaces within larger resort

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Forward momentum with rising y-position, trick input window open
Uncontrolled tumble animation, loss of player input responsiveness
Sprite animation matching event type, occasional collision with obstacles matching player vulnerability
Increasing horizontal scroll speed, terrain parallax acceleration
Arm raise animation, projectile arc trajectory visible on-screen

notes: Ski or Die operates as a minigame collection with minimal persistent world-state or faction complexity. Manifestation layer primarily conveys immediate sport-event status through animation frames and HUD elements rather than systemic political/danger signaling. Resort setting via Rodney Dangerfield character provides minimal narrative framing. Visual communication is functional—danger signals tied directly to collision/wipeout failure states, safety signals to smooth progression. No faction conflict or political situation indicated in available sources; competitive context is recreational sports multiplayer framing.
